sources.sourceName: Introduction to Sociology, OpenStax 3e (Pressbooks adaptation) - Appendix: Chapter Quiz Answers (odd-numbered)

meta.notes: Sociology (PSBB) items scraped verbatim from OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e Ch. 18 and Ch. 19 section-quiz pages; correct answers from the OpenStax-derived odd-numbered answer key (Pressbooks CC BY 4.0 adaptation). Ch. 18 Q17 was excluded because its keyed answer ('outsourcing') conflicts with the phenomenon described in the stem (job polarization), an apparent answer-key error. Live OpenStax page shows CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 plus a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing/non-AI per PRD.

questions.stem: Which of these is an example of a commodity?

choices.label: D
choices.text: A book, blog entry, or magazine article

questions.explanation: A commodity is a tangible, interchangeable good traded in the marketplace; corn is a classic example, whereas the other options are services or information.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 18 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 18 Q1 = B.

questions.stem: What is the most important commodity in a postindustrial society?
questions.explanation: Postindustrial (information) societies are organized around the production and exchange of information as their most important commodity.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 18 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 18 Q3 = C.

questions.stem: Which is an economic policy based on national policies of accumulating silver and gold by controlling markets with colonies and other countries through taxes and customs charges?
questions.explanation: Mercantilism is the economic policy of accumulating precious metals by controlling trade with colonies and other nations through tariffs and customs charges.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 18 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 18 Q5 = C.

questions.stem: The type of socialism now carried on by Russia is a form of ______ socialism.
questions.explanation: Per the source, Russia's contemporary system is described as a form of market socialism, which combines social ownership with market mechanisms.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 18 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 18 Q7 = B.

questions.stem: Which country serves as an example of convergence?
questions.explanation: Convergence theory predicts that developing economies will come to resemble industrialized ones; Singapore is cited as an example of such rapid convergence.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 18 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 18 Q9 = A.

questions.stem: Which of the following is not an aspect of globalization?

choices.label: D
choices.text: Integrating child care through international trade

questions.explanation: Globalization integrates governments, cultures, and finance through international trade; 'integrating child care' is not one of its aspects.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 18 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 18 Q11 = D.

questions.stem: All of the following are characteristics of global cities, except:
questions.explanation: Global cities are defined by economic and political functions (corporate HQs, political influence, NGO headquarters); hosting influential philosophers is not a defining characteristic.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 18 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 18 Q13 = D.

questions.stem: Which is evidence that the United States workforce is largely a meritocracy?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Job opportunities are increasing for highly skilled jobs.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Job opportunities are decreasing for midlevel jobs.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Highly skilled jobs pay better than low-skill jobs.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Women tend to make less than men do for the same job.

questions.explanation: Meritocracy implies rewards track skill and effort; the fact that highly skilled jobs pay better than low-skill jobs is evidence consistent with a meritocracy.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 18 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 18 Q15 = C.

questions.stem: Who determines which illnesses are stigmatized?
questions.explanation: Stigma is socially constructed: society collectively determines which illnesses carry stigma.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 19 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 19 Q1 = C.

questions.stem: The Rating of Perceived Exertion (RPE) is an example of ________________
questions.explanation: Because perceived exertion depends on subjective, individually and socially shaped judgments, the RPE illustrates the social construction of health.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 19 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 19 Q3 = A.

questions.stem: Core nations are also known as __________________
questions.explanation: In world-systems and global health frameworks, core nations are the wealthy, industrialized high-income nations.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 19 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 19 Q5 = A.

questions.stem: According to the World Health Organization, what was the most frequent cause of death for children under five in low-income countries?
questions.explanation: Per the WHO, pneumonia and diarrheal diseases are leading causes of death among children under five in low-income countries.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 19 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 19 Q7 = C.

questions.stem: The process by which aspects of life that were considered bad or deviant are redefined as sickness and needing medical attention to remedy is called:
questions.explanation: Medicalization is the process by which previously nonmedical problems (often framed as deviance) come to be defined and treated as medical conditions.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 19 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 19 Q9 = B.

questions.stem: Sidewalk ramps and Braille signs are examples of _______________.

choices.label: B
choices.text: accommodations required by the Americans with Disabilities Act

choices.label: C
choices.text: forms of accessibility for people with disabilities

questions.explanation: Sidewalk ramps and Braille signs are both ADA-required accommodations and forms of accessibility for people with disabilities, so 'both b and c' is correct.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 19 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 19 Q11 = D.

questions.stem: Which public healthcare system offers insurance primarily to people over sixty-five years old?
questions.explanation: Medicare is the U.S. public health insurance program primarily for people aged 65 and older. (Medicaid serves low-income people of all ages.)
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 19 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 19 Q13 = B.

questions.stem: What does the individual mandate provision of the 2010 U.S. healthcare reform do?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Requires everyone to buy insurance from the government

choices.label: B
choices.text: Requires everyone to sign up for Medicaid

choices.label: C
choices.text: Requires everyone to have insurance or pay a penalty

questions.explanation: The individual mandate of the Affordable Care Act required individuals to have health insurance or pay a tax penalty.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 19 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 19 Q15 = C.

questions.stem: What group created the Millennium Development Goals?
questions.explanation: Per the source, the World Health Organization is credited with creating the Millennium Development Goals related to global health.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 19 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 19 Q17 = D.

questions.stem: The class, race, and gender inequalities in our healthcare system support the _____________ perspective.
questions.explanation: Class, race, and gender inequalities in healthcare access and outcomes support the conflict perspective, which emphasizes structured inequality.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 19 Section Quiz
